recentHighlights: The Cloud (S1E06) — Voyager enters what it believes is a nebula, only to discover it is a living space creature. The encounter frames Janeway's leadership arc: her distance from the crew (Kim and Paris debating captain protocol, Neelix appointing himself morale officer) resolved through a pool game in Sandrine's. The Doctor's suture proposal saves the creature. The Flask as conductor, not container.

Phage (S1E05) — Neelix loses his lungs to the Vidiian Sodality. Janeway refuses to kill to save him, breaking the cycle of violence. The Doctor creates holographic lungs as his first true medical miracle. Kes donates a lung and begins training as his assistant. The episode establishes Voyager’s moral baseline: ethics are absolute, not situational, even at 70,000 light-years from home.

Time and Again (S1E04) — A temporal paradox episode. Janeway and Paris fall into the past and accidentally cause the disaster they came to investigate. The only resolution is a reset—the clean-stop discipline extended to causality itself. Kes feels the annihilation she didn’t experience, carrying a ghost memory of lives that were and weren’t lost.

Parallax (S1E03) — The first post-pilot episode. Trapped in a quantum singularity, Voyager is forced to face its own reflection—Maquis and Starfleet literally staring across a temporal gap at distorted versions of themselves. B’Elanna Torres is promoted to Chief Engineer. The Chapman letter scene proves that support follows you even across ruptures.
